author: omerfirmak
content: I believe it is just due to the order of checks in the spec, which results in a false depedency on the authority account.

author: m0_ox
content: All gas checks are performed before the balance is assessed - in yellow paper also I believe this is true. This is the reason it's still in BAL because these were touched when assessing the gas.

author: m0_ox
content: OOG / exceptional halt check on the instruction takes precedence to insufficient funds / balance check

author: omerfirmak
content: Yes, but reading the delegated account's state and it's code is not a part of gas checks, no?

author: omerfirmak
content: Only checking if it is warm/cold is required for gas checks

author: m0_ox
content: It would create some efficiencies I think, though, if we could check balance earlier

author: m0_ox
content: I see, let me check the exact test case you referenced

author: omerfirmak
content:
my impl works something like

```diff
diff --git a/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/system.py b/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/system.py
index 14a8bd133..00f37d267 100644
--- a/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/system.py
+++ b/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/system.py
@@ -455,9 +455,6 @@ def call(evm: Evm) -> None:
         if code_address not in evm.accessed_addresses:
             evm.accessed_addresses.add(code_address)
 
-    code_hash = get_account(tx_state, code_address).code_hash
-    code = get_code(tx_state, code_hash)
-
     # TODO: Consider consolidating charge_gas + charge_state_gas into
     # a single gas charge to avoid duplicate EVM trace entries.
     # Applies here and in create, create2, selfdestruct. See #2526.
@@ -491,6 +488,9 @@ def call(evm: Evm) -> None:
         evm.gas_left += message_call_gas.sub_call
         evm.state_gas_left += call_state_gas_reservoir
     else:
+        code_hash = get_account(tx_state, code_address).code_hash
+        code = get_code(tx_state, code_hash)
+
         generic_call(
             evm,
             message_call_gas.sub_call,
```

author: omerfirmak
content: but for all CALL* opcodes, obviously

author: m0_ox
content: I think there is some reasoning here that we charged the gas for the access, therefore we have accessed it. But technically you don't need to do so if there isn't enough funds, you only need to assess whether there is enough for COLD / WARM and if it can succeed, only then do you need to actually load it.

author: m0_ox
content: I'm not sure if this exact case was discussed, but I believe the reason is something like "we charged for access therefore we access it". I think there is room for making this optimization for sure... but we'd have to all agree on this.

author: omerfirmak
content: Yeah, of course, I just wanted to bring it up.

author: m0_ox
content: It's a good point. I think it's worth discussing.
